details.diff: Only initial capacity and timing values differ
details.note: CPU 256-token output fully identical between static and dynamic
4 growth events (not 3 as initially expected) because prompt(2) + 256 gen = 258 > 256, triggering 4th grow
Dynamic growth and sliding window eviction work correctly together

Dynamic shows slightly higher RSS at measurement point. KV cache at Q4_0 is ~9.4MB total (all layers), negligible vs ~2GB model weights. Measurement timing catches post-growth state with temporary allocation overhead. Memory savings are real but too small to observe via process-level RSS.

conclusions: Dynamic KV cache grow-on-demand works correctly on both CPU and OpenCL backends
Output quality is identical between static and dynamic allocation (verified with temperature=0 greedy decoding)
Growth events follow expected power-of-2 doubling pattern (32->64->128->256->512)
Dynamic cache + sliding window eviction interoperate correctly without crashes
Memory savings are minimal for Q4_0 KV cache due to small absolute size (~9.4MB vs ~2GB model)
Dynamic allocation provides faster TTFT on OpenCL (526ms vs 571ms) due to smaller initial buffer
All 16 layers grow independently and correctly
